title: Sapium mannianum
taxonomy: Sapium mannianum is classified within the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the spurge family. This family comprises a large number of species, including those with medicinal, ornamental, and edible properties. Within Euphorbiaceae, Sapium mannianum is often placed in the tribe Sapiineae, which is a group of flowering plants native to various regions around the world. intro: Sapium mannianum, also known as the Indian tinswood or Indian mahua, is a flowering plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Sapium mannianum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
